headline: Super Micro Co-Founder Charged in China AI Tech Case
summary: US prosecutors charged Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw in a scheme to send high-performance computer servers assembled in the US with sophisticated AI capabilities to China in violation of US export controls.  Liaw and others at the company allegedly sold the AI tech through a Southeast Asia company knowing it would be sent on to China.

content.title: Super Micro Co-Founder Charged in China AI Tech Case
content.summary: US prosecutors charged Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw in a scheme to send high-performance computer servers assembled in the US with sophisticated AI capabilities to China in violation of US export controls.  Liaw and others at the company allegedly sold the AI tech through a Southeast Asia company knowing it would be sent on to China.

headline: Super Micro Computer Employees Arrested for Alleged Sales to China
summary: Super Micro Computer placed two employees on leave and fired a contractor after charges of diverting U.S.-assembled servers to China, violating export control laws.

content.title: Super Micro Computer Employees Arrested for Alleged Sales to China
content.summary: Super Micro Computer placed two employees on leave and fired a contractor after charges of diverting U.S.-assembled servers to China, violating export control laws.

headline: Super Micro Co-Founder Charged With Sending AI Tech to China
summary: US prosecutors charged Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw in a scheme to send US-assembled servers containing Nvidia’s cutting-edge chips to China in violation of US export controls.  Liaw and two others associated with the company allegedly sold the AI tech through a Southeast Asia company knowing it would be sent on to China.

content.title: Super Micro Co-Founder Charged With Sending AI Tech to China
content.summary: US prosecutors charged Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw in a scheme to send US-assembled servers containing Nvidia’s cutting-edge chips to China in violation of US export controls.  Liaw and two others associated with the company allegedly sold the AI tech through a Southeast Asia company knowing it would be sent on to China.

headline: Supermicro’s co-founder was just arrested for allegedly smuggling $2.5 billion in GPUs to China
summary: The arrest of board member Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw—who previously resigned following an accounting scandal in 2018—led an after-hours trading drop of 12% in the Nvidia-linked server manufacturer's stock.

content.title: Supermicro’s co-founder was just arrested for allegedly smuggling $2.5 billion in GPUs to China
content.summary: The arrest of board member Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw—who previously resigned following an accounting scandal in 2018—led an after-hours trading drop of 12% in the Nvidia-linked server manufacturer's stock.

thumbnail.caption: Supermicro Chairman and CEO Charles Liang was not named in the indictment, nor was Supermicro, the company said in a statement.

headline: Super Micro Indictment Puts Focus On Compliance Risks And Valuation Gap
summary: US authorities have indicted a co founder of Super Micro Computer and several employees for alleged export control violations tied to AI capable servers shipped to China. The individuals are accused of sending advanced server systems to restricted Chinese customers in breach of US export laws. Super Micro Computer is not named as a defendant, and the company has placed the implicated employees on leave and ended work with a contractor while cooperating with investigators. For investors...

headline: Super Micro Tanks 22% as Co-Founder Arrested in $2.5 Billion AI Chip Smuggling Ring

headline: Super Micro shares plunge as US charges co-founder, 2 more for smuggling AI chips to China
summary: Super Micro shares sank 24% on Friday after U.S. prosecutors charged three people linked with the company, including its co-founder, ‌with helping smuggle billions of dollars worth of AI technology to ‌China.  The drop in premarket trading would erase more than $4 billion from Super Micro's $18.49 billion market value ​if the losses hold.  U.S. prosecutors did not name Super Micro - a major AI server builder using Nvidia's chips - in the complaint.

content.title: Super Micro shares plunge as US charges co-founder, 2 more for smuggling AI chips to China
content.summary: Super Micro shares sank 24% on Friday after U.S. prosecutors charged three people linked with the company, including its co-founder, ‌with helping smuggle billions of dollars worth of AI technology to ‌China.  The drop in premarket trading would erase more than $4 billion from Super Micro's $18.49 billion market value ​if the losses hold.  U.S. prosecutors did not name Super Micro - a major AI server builder using Nvidia's chips - in the complaint.
